title: Networks and the Web
description: How networks connect devices, how packets travel across the Internet via TCP/IP, and how HTTP, DNS and other protocols make the World Wide Web work.
objectives: Distinguish LAN and WAN, and compare star and bus network topologies
Describe the roles of key network hardware: switches, routers and NICs
Compare the client-server and peer-to-peer network models
Explain how packet switching breaks data into packets that travel independently and are reassembled
Describe the layers of the TCP/IP stack and the distinct roles of TCP and IP within it
Identify key application-layer protocols (HTTP/HTTPS, FTP, SMTP) and the role of DNS, and distinguish the Internet from the World Wide Web

lessons.title: Packet switching and the TCP/IP stack
Protocols, DNS, and the Internet vs the Web
